title: Auto-referencing in digital holographic microscopy reconstruction
abstract: A computer-implemented method for analyzing digital holographic microscopy (DHM) data for hematology applications includes receiving a DHM image acquired using a digital holographic microscopy system. The DHM image comprises depictions of one or more cell objects and background. A reference image is generated based on the DHM image. This reference image may then be used to reconstruct a fringe pattern in the DHM image into an optical depth map.
claims: Claims 1. A computer-implemented method for analyzing digital holographic microscopy (DHM) data for hematology applications, the method comprising: receiving a DHM image acquired using a digital holographic microscopy system, the DHM image comprising depictions of one or more cell objects and background; generating a reference image using the DHM image; and using the reference image to reconstruct a fringe pattern in the DHM image into an optical depth map. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the reference image is generated by: detecting one or more regions in the DHM image where a cell object is present; segmenting the one or more regions from the DHM image; and fill in the one or more regions of the DHM image with an undisturbed fringe pattern from the background to yield the reference image. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein the one or more regions are detected automatically using a learning-based method. 4. The method of claim 2, wherein the one or more regions each comprise a box completely surrounding each cell object. 5. The method of claim 2, wherein a dictionary learning method is used to fill in the one or more regions of the DHM image. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein the dictionary learning method comprises: learning a dictionary of image patches that capture variability in the background; and re-projecting the DHM image using the dictionary of image patches to fill in the one or more regions. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein sparse coding is used to generate the dictionary of image patches. 8. The method of claim 2, wherein a compressed sensing method is used to fill in the one or more regions of the DHM image. 9. The method of claim 2 wherein an image in-painting method is used to fill in the one or more regions of the DHM image based on structure and texture information included in the DHM image. 10. A computer-implemented method for analyzing digital holographic microscopy (DHM) data for hematology applications, the method comprising: receiving a plurality of DHM images acquired using a digital holographic microscopy system, each DHM image comprising depictions of one or more cell objects and background; generating a reference image based on the plurality of DHM images comprising only contributions from the background of the plurality of DHM images; using the reference image to reconstruct a fringe pattern in the DHM image into an optical depth map. 11. The method of claim 10, wherein the reference image is generated by: applying one or more filtering algorithms to dynamically estimate the reference image. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein the plurality of DHM images are received in succession and the one or more filtering algorithms are applied to each DHM image by: updating a previously generated reference image by applying a filter to the DHM image and a predetermined number of previously received DHM images to yield a new reference image. 13. The method of claim 12, wherein the filter comprises a moving average filter. 14. The method of claim 12, wherein the filter comprises a Gaussian filter. 15. The method of claim 12, wherein the filter comprises a median filter. 16. The method of claim 12, wherein the predetermined number of previously received DHM images is selected by a user. 17. The method of claim 11, wherein applying the one or more filtering algorithms comprises: computing intensity statistics for each pixel region across the plurality of DHM images; selecting stable distribution values corresponding to the background from the intensity statistics. 18. The method of claim 10, further comprising: receiving an object- free original reference image, wherein the reference image is generated by modifying the object- free original reference image to reflect changes in imaging conditions occurring in the plurality of DHM images. 19. The method of claim 10, wherein generating of the reference image is triggered by switching from one patient blood sample to another with a microfluidic or spotting control unit. 20. The method of claim 10, wherein the reference image is generated a user-specified time intervals and reconstruction of the fringe pattern in the DHM image into the optical depth map is performed at using a most recently computed reference image. 21. The method of claim 10, further comprising: generating a real-time visualization of one or more fringe-free reconstructed phase images using the plurality of DHM images and the fringe pattern. 22. The method of claim 10, further comprising: generating a 3-D visualization of a cell surface using the plurality of DHM images and the fringe pattern. 23. A system for analyzing digital holographic microscopy (DHM) data for hematology applications, the system comprising: a DHM system configured to acquire a plurality of DHM images, each DHM image comprising depictions of one or more cell objects and background; one or more processors; a non-transitory, computer-readable storage medium in operable communication with the processors, wherein the computer-readable storage medium comprises one or more programming instructions that, when executed, cause the processors to: generate a reference image comprising only contributions from the background of the plurality of DHM images; and use the reference image to reconstruct a fringe pattern in the DHM image into an optical depth map. 24. The system of claim 23, wherein the one or more processors comprise a plurality of graphical processing units. 25. The system of claim 23, wherein the one or more processors further comprise a plurality of central processing units.
